title: daemon/jmap.rs: session_state_token reads are non-transactional
description:
daemon/src/jmap.rs session_state_token(): assembles a composite state token from 7 sequential SELECT COUNT/MAX queries with no surrounding transaction. A write landing between any two reads produces a token that reflects mixed snapshot versions. A client that receives this token and later uses it in a /changes request may receive a "cannotCalculateChanges" error or miss updates.

Root cause: RFC 8620 §2 requires the session state to reflect a consistent point-in-time snapshot; reading counts and sequences in separate statements does not guarantee this.

Fix: wrap all 7 reads in a single BEGIN DEFERRED ... COMMIT (read-only snapshot transaction) so they all see the same database state.
